frameworks.name: scale-invariant temporal representation
frameworks.one_line: logarithmically compressed timeline of past and future events

frameworks.name: reinforcement learning
frameworks.one_line: extends RL value estimation beyond exponential discounting to power-law

frameworks.name: model-based reinforcement learning
frameworks.one_line: criticized for linear computational cost in simulated time

frameworks.name: model-free reinforcement learning
frameworks.one_line: criticized for requiring a fixed exponential discount timescale

core_question: How can a natural learner compute a scale-invariant estimate of future outcomes in continuous time without committing to a single discount timescale?
key_assumptions: future time can be represented on a logarithmically compressed scale
the whole timeline is constructible in a single parallel operation
manipulations: contrast with step-wise model-based transition estimation
key_findings: proposed mechanism efficiently estimates future inputs as a function of future time on a log-compressed scale
mechanism yields a scale-invariant power-law-discounted estimate of expected future reward
entire future timeline is constructed in a single parallel operation
interpretations: scale-invariant timeline avoids the timescale-selection drawback of exponential discounting and the linear cost drawback of step-wise simulation
the mechanism generates concrete behavioral and neural predictions and could be incorporated into future RL algorithms
